1.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
1.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;
probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

2.gl: peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
2.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
2.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);
probably from G939 (βάσις) (through the notion of a foundation of power);

3.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
3.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

14.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)

19.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
19.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;
from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure);

21.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
to think differently or afterwards, i.e. reconsider (morally, feel compunction)

25.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
25.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

26.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

28.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
28.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,
